overview.description: AI model selection guidance for each phase and task of cloud deployment optimization. Models are chosen based on task complexity, required reasoning depth, and cost-effectiveness.

sonnet.strengths: Fast, cost-effective, excellent at code generation, config changes, and well-defined transformations
sonnet.best_for: Straightforward implementation tasks with clear acceptance criteria

opus.strengths: Deep reasoning, complex system design, edge case analysis, LLM integration
opus.best_for: Architecture decisions, LLM function calling schema, security-sensitive code

haiku.best_for: Not recommended for this feature — tasks require code understanding beyond haiku's capability

phase_model_assignments.name: Foundation & Memory Safety
phase_model_assignments.rationale: Tasks are straightforward config/value changes. CHROMIUM_LAUNCH_ARGS editing, systemd memory limits, and .dockerignore creation are well-defined transformations with clear acceptance criteria. No creative reasoning needed.

phase_model_assignments.name: Docker Multi-Stage Build
phase_model_assignments.rationale: Dockerfile is template-driven with well-known patterns (multi-stage build, Playwright Chromium installation). The guide already provides a reference Dockerfile. Sonnet can adapt it to the project's actual structure.
phase_model_assignments.notes: If Playwright system dependencies fail on Alpine (rare), escalate to Opus for troubleshooting.
phase_model_assignments.estimated_cost: Low-Medium — Dockerfile creation and build verification
phase_model_assignments.context_requirements: package.json (for scripts), server.ts (for entry point), config/env.ts (for env vars)

phase_model_assignments.name: Deploy Safety & Data Persistence
phase_model_assignments.rationale: Shell script modifications (release pruning, health check verification) are well-defined. The existing deploy-release.sh and rollback-release.sh provide clear insertion points.

phase_model_assignments.name: SQLite & I/O Optimization
phase_model_assignments.rationale: PRAGMA application is straightforward — add 6 pragmas at DB open. Activity log capping is a simple array slice operation. Both are well-documented patterns.
phase_model_assignments.notes: If SQLite WAL mode causes issues on NFS (rare edge case), escalate to Opus for lock analysis.

phase_model_assignments.name: Observability & Health Enrichment
phase_model_assignments.rationale: Periodic GC interval, health endpoint enrichment, and express.static cache headers are straightforward additions. lib/resourceMonitor.ts already exposes metrics — just wire them into the route.

phase_model_assignments.name: NL Webhook LLM Router (Optional)
phase_model_assignments.rationale: This phase involves LLM function calling, which requires: (1) designing a robust tool schema that handles ambiguous natural language, (2) implementing error handling for malformed LLM responses, (3) integrating with external APIs (Gemini), (4) security-sensitive HMAC verification. These require deep reasoning about edge cases and failure modes.

rationale_by_task.T-025: Tool schema design requires anticipating ambiguous user intents and edge cases in natural language — Opus for robust schema
rationale_by_task.T-026: LLM integration with error handling, retry logic, and fallback behavior — Opus for system design
rationale_by_task.T-027: HMAC verification is a standard cryptographic pattern — Sonnet can implement from test reference
rationale_by_task.T-028: Rate limiting addition is straightforward middleware — Sonnet

phase_model_assignments.estimated_cost: Medium-High — LLM integration + external API calls + testing
phase_model_assignments.context_requirements: routes/api/nl.ts, config/env.ts, tests/integration/nlWebhook.test.ts, lib/sharedBrowser.ts (for runtimeControls access)

sonnet_expected_performance.code_edit_accuracy: > 95% — edits compile and pass tests on first attempt
sonnet_expected_performance.template_adaptation: > 90% — Dockerfile, shell scripts adapted correctly from reference

opus_expected_performance.schema_design_accuracy: > 98% — tool schemas handle edge cases without rework
opus_expected_performance.integration_complexity_handling: > 95% — LLM integration handles failure modes correctly

cost_optimization.total_estimated_tokens: ~500K input tokens for Phases 1-5 (Sonnet), ~100K input tokens for Phase 6 (Opus)
cost_saving_strategies: Use Sonnet for all straightforward tasks — only escalate to Opus for LLM integration
Reuse context across tasks within a phase — avoid re-reading the same files
For verification-only tasks (T-003, T-011, T-020), use Haiku or Bash directly — no AI reasoning needed
Cache Docker build layers — reference Dockerfile in guide reduces Sonnet's research time

escalate_to_opus_when: Sonnet's implementation fails tests after 2 attempts
Task involves LLM API integration or function calling
New edge case discovered that changes the spec
Docker build fails due to non-obvious system dependency issues
degrade_to_sonnet_when: Opus is unavailable or rate-limited (fallback for any task)
Task is a simple config change or value edit
avoid_haiku_for: Any task requiring understanding of project architecture
Any task with acceptance criteria beyond syntax checking
